reference_images.role: primary pixel-art rendering and palette anchor
visually inspected character consistency anchor; not submitted because the static player image already represents the same design
secondary outline, texture, and survival-wear anchor
low-saturation world palette and ground-contrast anchor

reference_observations: Character art uses chunky readable pixels, strong dark outlines, restrained highlights, and black-gray, olive, khaki, brown, and muted red accents.
The map is low-contrast gray-green asphalt with sparse khaki and dark-red details, so pickup identities need a clean silhouette and selective light accents.
All generated references were used only for style and palette; no character, enemy, map, road, or blood element appears in the candidates.

candidates.visual_review: Strong standard-can silhouette, clear open-top cue, restrained wear, and stable burgundy-charcoal-khaki identity. It matches the reference outline weight and remains the more distinctive small icon candidate.
Clean slimmer alternative with a restrained abstract panel, but the left contour has more abrupt stepped protrusions and the body is less distinctive than candidate A at small size.

assets.recommendation_reason: Candidate A has the stronger compact silhouette, clearer material separation, and more robust survival-loot identity while remaining text-free and brand-free.
risks: The generated source is 1254x1254 rather than the later 256x256 master canvas; crop, padding, and nearest-neighbor reduction must be handled only after batch approval.
At 32x32 the top opening and khaki accents may need deterministic pixel cleanup; no such derived or final asset was produced in this work package.
The dark body must be checked against the actual map at world scale before publication.

candidates.visual_review: Detailed olive field pouch with excellent zipper and handle readability. The nested off-white and green plus treatment is visually busier and less direct than candidate B.
Broad, readable 2x2 silhouette with a single unambiguous muted-green plus on an off-white patch, strong khaki-olive separation, and no red medical emblem.

assets.recommendation_reason: Candidate B communicates the medical identity most directly at small size and better matches the requested white-and-green ordinary medical mark without invoking a red-cross emblem.
risks: The left fabric loop and lower-right zipper pull widen the silhouette; final 512x512 master framing must preserve the required safety margin.
The generated source is 1254x1254 rather than the later 512x512 master canvas; deterministic crop and reduction remain pending batch approval.
The khaki body needs a world-scale map preview to confirm contrast on gray-green ground.
